top_signals.title: Hackers abuse ViPNet software to target Russian govt agencies
top_signals.summary: An advanced threat actor is abusing the update mechanism for the ViPNet private networking product suite to target Russian organizations, including government agencies. [...]

top_signals.title: Top China, Philippines envoys to meet for first time since 2024
top_signals.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi is scheduled to meet with Philippine counterpart Theresa Lazaro on the sidelines of the ASEAN foreign ministers meeting in Manila.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Allbridge pauses cross-chain bridge after $1.65M exploit
top_signals.summary: The attacker allegedly used a flash loan and rapid swaps to manipulate the bridge’s stablecoin exchange rate.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: US service member killed in northern Iraq, US Central Command says
top_signals.summary: A U.S. service member was killed on Saturday “during a controlled detonation of unexploded ordnance” in northern Iraq, CENTCOM confirmed.

top_signals.title: FTSE 100 property giant Segro rejects £13.5bn Prologis bid
top_signals.summary: Segro has rejected a second and third takeover bid from US rival Prologis, as the tussle between the two property giants steps up a gear. The FTSE 100 real estate firm rejected Prologis’ latest bid last week, which amounted to £9.93 per share and valued the group’s total shares at £13.5bn.  Prologis said its latest [...]

top_signals.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, May 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. goods and services trade deficit increased in May 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit increased from $54.6 billion in April (revised) to $77.6 billion in May, as exports decreased and imports increased. The goods deficit increased $23.6 billion in May to $106.5 billion. The services surplus increased $0.6 billion in May to $28.9 billion. Full Text
